task_completion.description: Weasis is open and displaying a DICOM image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis application window is visible with a medical CT scan loaded in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: An arrow annotation is drawn on the DICOM image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graphical arrow is visible overlaid on the medical image, pointing to a specific anatomical structure.

task_completion.description: A text annotation reading 'Suspected lesion' is added to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Suspected lesion' is visible as an annotation overlaid on the medical image.

task_completion.description: The export or save dialog is opened in Weasis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for exporting or saving the image view is visible over the Weasis interface.

task_completion.description: The export dialog is configured to save a JPEG to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The export dialog shows the file format as JPEG (.jpg) and the destination path as ~/DICOM/exports/annotated_finding.jpg or /home/ga/DICOM/exports/annotated_finding.jpg.

task_completion.description: The annotated image is successfully exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save/export dialog is confirmed (e.g., clicking 'Export' or 'Save'), or a file manager/terminal shows the newly created annotated_finding.jpg file in the correct directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Weasis GUI to create annotations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking annotation tools in the Weasis toolbar and interacting with the image canvas, rather than using terminal commands (like ImageMagick) to edit the image.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's export feature rather than taking a screenshot.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent navigates the Weasis menu (e.g., File -> Export) to save the image, rather than using a system screenshot tool to capture the screen.

integrity.description: The export settings indicate annotations are burned into the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the export dialog, options related to exporting the 'view' (which burns in overlays) rather than raw DICOM data are selected, ensuring the annotations are part of the final JPEG.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected export path is exactly '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/annotated_finding.jpg'. The text annotation must be exactly 'Suspected lesion'. The exported file must be a valid JPEG (expected to be >10,000 bytes, indicating a real image export rather than an empty file).
